title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_67 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 67 the four-class F25 line appears to admit a much cleaner late-regime witness than the earlier spill-management feeders: A can be a 20-label 2,3-smooth reservoir, B can be a zero-spill high barrier above N/2, and C can be exactly the remaining 66 primes greater than 3. The barrier side disappears completely, so the only live arithmetic burden is complementary-prime-pool supply.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for the exact instance Γ(Z_67 × Z_25) survives bounded prior-art audit and direct recomputation on the full 354-vertex graph. The result is verified instance-level evidence for the live zero-spill complementary-prime-pool F25 campaign slice, but it is not yet Lean-complete and not yet a quantified family theorem.
candidate_theorem_slice: A late zero-spill F25 slice: if N = 5p + 19 admits 20 labels supported on the prime palette {2,3}, three distinct barrier primes in (N/2, N], and at least p - 1 further primes greater than 3 outside that barrier, then taking A from the 2,3-smooth reservoir, B = {1,b1,b2,b3}, C from the complementary prime pool, and D as the complement yields a prime labeling of Γ(Z_p × Z_25).
next_action: Promote this verified p = 67 feeder into the zero-spill complementary-prime-pool family dossier and use Γ(Z_71 × Z_25) as the next bounded stress test; Lean is reasonable for the exact witness only if the campaign wants another preserved feeder artifact.
